name: Fuglede's Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the Hilbert space H and its bounded linear operators B(H)

VINIT.justification: void state before any relationship is asserted between T and U; the theorem begins with two operators in isolation

TANCH.chosen_element: the Hilbert space H with its complete inner product structure
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains and closes the entire system of operator relationships; the universal container for all bounded operators

AFWD.chosen_element: the forward implication: if T is normal, then T admits spectral decomposition
AFWD.justification: directed transformation toward the target state; normality (TT* = T*T) morphs into spectral structure

AREV.chosen_element: the reverse implication: if T admits spectral decomposition, then T is normal
AREV.justification: contravariant descent; spectral form reconstructs the normality condition; the backward path from conclusion to premise

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of the forward and reverse implications into a biconditional equivalence
CLINK.justification: sequential chaining: T normal ↔ T has spectral decomposition; the two morphisms compose into a complete characterization

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the identity operator I on H, and the self-adjoint property T = T* (for self-adjoint T)
IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference and self-recognition; the operator recognizes itself through its own adjoint; identity is the fixed point of the spectral theorem

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the branching of a normal operator T into its spectral decomposition: T = ∫ λ dE(λ)
FSPLIT.justification: co-multiplication δ: one operator splits into a continuum of eigenvalue-eigenprojection pairs; the single object branches into its spectral components

FFUSE.chosen_element: the reconstruction of T from its spectral measure: T = ∫ λ dE(λ) → T
FFUSE.justification: multiplication μ: the spectral components reconstitute the original operator exactly; FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T holds by the spectral theorem

EVALT.chosen_element: the affirmative case: T is normal (TT* = T*T)
EVALT.justification: true/affirmative branch; the condition is satisfied; the operator admits full spectral analysis

EVALF.chosen_element: the negative case: T is not normal (TT* ≠ T*T)
EVALF.justification: false/negative branch; the normality condition fails; spectral decomposition into eigenvalues does not exist in the classical sense

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the paradice of approximate normality: an operator T that is 'nearly normal' or satisfies normality in a weak or limiting sense
ENGAGR.justification: both simultaneously held without resolution; neither fully normal nor fully non-normal; the operator exhibits both spectral and non-spectral properties in superposition

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent record of the spectral decomposition: E(λ) the projection-valued measure, fixed once computed
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation, append-only; once the spectral measure is determined, it cannot be undone; it is the irreversible witness to the operator's structure

phase_2.split_input: T (a single normal operator on H)
split_outputs: spectral measure E(λ) (projection-valued measure on ℝ)
eigenvalue continuum λ ∈ σ(T) (spectrum of T)
phase_2.fuse_result: T = ∫ λ dE(λ) (the original operator, recovered exactly)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no operator defined; no relationship asserted; the theorem has not yet been invoked
phase_3.true_description: 01 — T is normal; spectral decomposition exists and is unique; the forward and reverse implications both hold
phase_3.false_description: 10 — T is not normal; classical spectral decomposition fails; the operator cannot be diagonalized by a unitary similarity
phase_3.both_description: 11 — a paradice: T exhibits both normal and non-normal properties simultaneously, or satisfies normality in a limiting or weak sense; the theorem is held in superposition

steps.domain_action: genesis of the operator pair (T, U) in uninitialized state; no properties yet asserted
establish the Hilbert space H as the universal container; all operators live within B(H)
branch the normal operator T into its spectral decomposition T = ∫ λ dE(λ); one object becomes a continuum of eigenvalue-projection pairs
affirm the true branch: T satisfies TT* = T*T (normality condition holds)
forward morphism along the true branch: normality implies the existence of a unique spectral measure E(λ)
compose the spectral measure with the eigenvalue integral: ∫ λ dE(λ) is well-defined and equals T
self-reference: the spectral decomposition recognizes T through its own adjoint T*; T = T* for self-adjoint T
permanently record the spectral measure E(λ); this decomposition is now fixed and irreversible
reconstitute T from its spectral components: ∫ λ dE(λ) → T; the branches fuse back into the original operator
affirm the false branch: T does not satisfy TT* = T*T (normality condition fails)
reverse morphism along the false branch: non-normality implies the absence of a complete spectral decomposition; descent into partial or generalized spectral theory
compose the reverse path: if spectral decomposition fails, then T cannot be normal; the contravariant chain closes
paradice state: an operator that is neither fully normal nor fully non-normal; both branches held simultaneously without resolution
self-reference loop: the operator recognizes itself through the paradice; identity is maintained even in superposition
final composition: the biconditional equivalence is complete: T is normal ↔ T admits spectral decomposition
close the boundary; the entire theorem is now contained within the Hilbert space structure; the system is sealed

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the spectral theorem itself; it translates the domain intention (characterize normal operators) into the operational sequence (normality ↔ spectral decomposition)
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the adjoint operation T ↔ T*; communication between an operator and its dual; the commutation relation [T, T*] = 0 is the message protocol
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the projection-valued measure E(λ); it stores the spectral information; the state of the system is encoded in the distribution of eigenvalues and their multiplicities
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the spectral ordering: operations proceed from normality check → spectral decomposition → reconstruction; the eigenvalue ordering λ₁ ≤ λ₂ ≤ ... determines the sequence
phase_5.alfs_store: the canonical reference is the spectral theorem for normal operators in Hilbert space; the bootstrap store is the set of all projection-valued measures on ℝ

phase_6.cycle_cost: the computational cost of spectral decomposition: O(dim H) for finite-dimensional H; for infinite-dimensional H, the cost is the measure-theoretic integration
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: system state before: T is an arbitrary bounded linear operator on H; no spectral information is known; entropy is maximal (operator is opaque)
phase_6.post_cycle_state: system state after: T is either confirmed normal (spectral decomposition computed) or confirmed non-normal (spectral decomposition fails); entropy is reduced (operator structure is revealed)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative because the spectral decomposition is a reversible transformation (FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T); information is reorganized but not lost; the Frobenius constraint ensures that the forward and reverse paths cancel entropically

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → IFIX → FFUSE → EVALF → AREV → CLINK → ENGAGR → IMSCRIB → CLINK → TANCH
-- Class: Fuglede's Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(10,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=16
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [7] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑚 → 𐑙  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑖  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑙 → 𐑗  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [10] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑖 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [11] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑳  | composition — regime coherence
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑱 → 𐑠  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑳 → 𐑱  | identity — self-imscription
--   [14] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑠 → 𐑡  | composition — regime coherence
--   [15] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑱 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def fugledes_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (genesis of the operator pair (T, U) in uninitialized state; no properties yet...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (establish the Hilbert space H as the universal container; all operators live ...)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (branch the normal operator T into its spectral decomposition T = ∫ λ dE(λ); one object becomes a continuum of eigenvalue-projection pairs) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (5 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (affirm the true branch: T satisfies TT* = T*T (normality condition holds))
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism along the true branch: normality implies the existence of a ...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the spectral measure with the eigenvalue integral: ∫ λ dE(λ) is well-...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference: the spectral decomposition recognizes T through its own adjoi...)
        (.arrow 𐑭 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (permanently record the spectral measure E(λ); this decomposition is now fixed...)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑖)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstitute T from its spectral components: ∫ λ dE(λ) → T; the branches fuse back into the original operator)
  (.arrow 𐑖 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [9] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (affirm the false branch: T does not satisfy TT* = T*T (normality condition fa...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑖 𐑱)  -- [10] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism along the false branch: non-normality implies the absence of...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑳)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose the reverse path: if spectral decomposition fails, then T cannot be n...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑱 𐑠)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (paradice state: an operator that is neither fully normal nor fully non-normal...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑳 𐑱)  -- [13] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference loop: the operator recognizes itself through the paradice; ide...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [14] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (final composition: the biconditional equivalence is complete: T is normal ↔ T...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [15] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (close the boundary; the entire theorem is now contained within the Hilbert sp...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def fugledes_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (fugledes_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def fugledes_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (fugledes_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem fugledes_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
